behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the core failure is not local parsing alone; it is whether the real speech model keeps speaker separation. REAL-INFRA: no OS/subprocess requirement beyond normal API call. MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP: a stub transcript with perfect speaker tags would always pass and hide diarization failures from the real model, so this must hit real model output. Rejected Workflow: behavior text focuses on preserving labels in STT output (single model-quality boundary), not a required chain of multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Send a real multi-speaker meeting clip to the production speech-to-text API and assert returned transcript segments include correct speaker labels for each utterance; then verify downstream attribution input built from that transcript maps statements to the labeled attendees.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is parser logic on transcript text and target structure, a single local boundary. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model judgment for pass/fail. Rejected Integration: behavior text is specifically about extraction logic, not cross-module state propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Call the task-assignment parser function with transcript fixtures containing explicit assignee/task phrases and assert emitted objects contain the correct assignee and task text fields in the summary-input schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in whether the summarizer actually produces complete attendee coverage, not just deterministic routing. REAL-INFRA: no special OS/subprocess boundary required. MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP: mocked summary output can be made perfect and will hide real omissions by the model. Rejected Workflow: this can be tested with one real summarization call using a prepared transcript, so multiple agentic stages are not strictly required.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript with known attendee-task assignments to the real summarization LLM and assert the structured output includes at least one action item for each attendee who received an assignment.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is deterministic routing of already-identified decisions into schema fields. REAL-INFRA: no real infra needed. LLM-DEP: correctness does not depend on real model output if inputs already contain identified decisions. Rejected Integration: no required cross-module failure boundary is stated; routing can fail within one mapper/formatter function.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the decision-routing mapper with fixtures containing identified decision entities and assert they populate the `decisions` schema section and are absent from generic narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this checks whether model output captures and places decisions correctly from natural language transcript content. REAL-INFRA: no mandatory OS/subprocess behavior beyond API call. MOCK-HIDE/LLM-DEP: deterministic stubs would hide extraction/placement misses from the real model. Rejected Workflow: one real summarization decision-extraction pass can validate this behavior without requiring multi-step agentic chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real summarization model on transcripts containing explicit decision statements and assert those decisions appear in the structured `decisions` section, with none only present in free-form narrative fields.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears across stage handoffs (split -> per-segment processing order -> merge). MULTI-STEP: behavior explicitly requires sequential processing of multiple segments, i.e., repeated model-driven stages and final merge coherence. LLM-DEP: coherence of merged summary depends on real model outputs across segments. REAL-INFRA may matter for large-file handling but is not the sole boundary. Rejected Agentic: behavior text requires a sequence of segment-level processing and merge, not a single model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the long-audio path with a real >60 minute recording so the system performs segment splitting, sequential segment transcription/summarization, and merge; assert final structured summary is coherent across segment boundaries (no dropped/duplicated segment content, consistent attendee/action/decision aggregation).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: includes real upload and external API interactions. LLM-DEP: includes real transcription and summarization model outputs. MULTI-STEP: explicit end-to-end chain across multiple stages with model-involved handoffs. Any lower level would miss failures in orchestration across steps. Rejected System: behavior is not only infra/process completion; it includes multi-agentic pipeline stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end test with a real meeting audio file through production-like pipeline (upload, STT, LLM summary, structured output delivery) and assert successful completion plus valid final schema without manual retries/intervention.
